review_items.notes: Standard clause. No HOA or third-party review bodies identified for this site.
Change order language is clear and consistent with Mexican contract law.
Clause adequately covers site condition discovery risk. Coastal zone conditions (SEMARNAT) are a known variable and should be referenced in client communications.
NOM-151-SCFI reference is current and appropriate. Electronic signature is valid.

ADVISORY (non-blocking): The proposal should clearly inform the client that the SEMARNAT coastal zone permit (ZOFEMAT) is a prerequisite for construction — not just a project phase. Recommend adding a plain-language note in the proposal that construction cannot begin without this permit. This is not a blocking condition but protects both parties.

advisory_notes: Add plain-language note to client proposal clarifying that the SEMARNAT coastal zone permit is a hard prerequisite for construction start — independent of design completion.

Payment schedule M3 (executive plans approved) triggers at design completion, before permit. Confirm client understands permit processing may extend total timeline by 3-6 months beyond design completion.
